step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("synarthroses examples")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed review of the retrieved evidence, I find support for all three options A, B, and C representing different types of synarthroses—sutures, syndesmoses, and gomphoses respectively. Each provides concrete examples illustrating key characteristics of synarthrotic joints including minimal to no movement permitted via firm attachment mechanisms such as fibrous tissues or specialized bony structures. Therefore, given that option D encompasses all these varied instances demonstrating the full spectrum of rigid joint configurations falling under the umbrella definition of synarthrosis, I concur wholeheartedly with the initial determination made earlier.
